title: Dockerfile Frontend, Build Context, Instructions, and Process Contracts · Release Workshop
summary: A platform pairing session must produce the first working reviewed application Dockerfile and build-context contract for dockerfile frontend, build context, instructions, and process contracts, with predictions recorded before each simulated build or lifecycle change. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of reviewed application Dockerfile and build-context contract while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case prac…
objectives: Use a pinned stable Dockerfile syntax frontend and explain parser directives, escape behavior, comments, continuations, and version boundaries.
Define local, Git, named, empty, and remote build contexts and use Dockerfile-specific ignore rules to minimize authority and invalidation.
Use FROM, WORKDIR, COPY, and justified ADD behavior with explicit ownership, paths, checksums, and context boundaries.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# docker-basics cumulative container delivery portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never connect to a Docker daemon, mount a socket, or execute learner commands.
workspace: docker
version-baseline:
  engine: 29.6.1
  compose: 5.3.1
  buildx: 0.35.0
  buildkit: 0.31.1
  dockerfile-frontend: 1.25
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-environment
evidence:

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Workshop Dockerfile Syntax Parser Directives
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop build sends a 780 MiB context containing .git and local.env, uses a floating base, copies the repository, runs as root, and wraps the process in /bin/sh. Redesign context and runtime contracts. Competency probe: Use a pinned stable Dockerfile syntax frontend and explain parser directives, escape behavior, comments, continuations, and version boundaries.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Commit to the mission model · Workshop Dockerfile Syntax Parser Directives.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for use a pinned stable dockerfile syntax frontend and explain parser directives, escape behavior, comments, continuations, and version boundaries. For Commit to the mission model · Workshop Dockerfile Syntax Parser Directives, keep Dockerfile Syntax Parser Directives connected to mechanically reviewable Docker artifact and transfer evidence.

content.text: A workshop build sends a 780 MiB context containing .git and local.env, uses a floating base, copies the repository, runs as root, and wraps the process in /bin/sh. Redesign context and runtime contracts. Competency probe: Use a pinned stable Dockerfile syntax frontend and explain parser directives, escape behavior, comments, continuations, and version boundaries.

content.title: Commit before feedback
content.text: Choose from the stated data before opening the worked model. Record which Dockerfile Syntax Parser Directives condition decided your prediction.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing
content.value: Dockerfile Reference and Building Best Practices — Controls parser directives, instructions, context, shell and exec forms, users, healthchecks, mounts, multi-stage builds, and Dockerfile behavior.

Redesign context and runtime contracts. steps.title: Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Dockerfile Cmd Entrypoint Contract
steps.instruction:
Append the Docker evidence item headed "  # Evidence: dockerfile-cmd-entrypoint-contract-175eh2". Tailor its Dockerfile change to the current stakeholder case, retain a deterministic review command, reject one concrete failure, and record a repair. The browser analyzes text only; run Docker commands later in an authorized disposable transfer environment.

Evidence target: Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Dockerfile Cmd Entrypoint Contract.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Dockerfile Cmd Entrypoint Contract into observable performance evidence. For Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Dockerfile Cmd Entrypoint Contract, keep Dockerfile Cmd Entrypoint Contract connected to mechanically reviewable Docker artifact and transfer evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: dockerfile-cmd-entrypoint-contract-175eh2
  - competency: dockerfile-cmd-entrypoint-contract
    artifact: Dockerfile
    decision: dockerfile cmd entrypoint contract
    scenario-variant: 5
    environment: multi-tenant build farm using an isolated BuildKit driver
    fault-injection: deny outbound DNS after initial service discovery
    retained-proof: per-platform manifest digest and selected image configuration
    case-key: 175eh2
    change: |
      FROM docker.io/library/alpine:3.22@sha256:verified AS runtime
      WORKDIR /app
      COPY --link public/ ./public/
      USER 10001:10001
      ENTRYPOINT ["/app/status"]
    verify: docker buildx build --check --progress=plain .
    failure: Reject a floating base, secret-bearing context, shell-form signal wrapper, root runtime, or copied repository wildcard.
    repair: Pin the reviewed base digest, shrink .dockerignore context, use explicit COPY, set numeric non-root identity, and test argv behavior.
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.text: The structural check is only one gate. The following changed-case decision and explanation must also hold.

content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Compose ENTRYPOINT and CMD into an override-friendly PID 1 contract and test arguments, signals, and exit status.

hints: For Defend the transferred model · Workshop Dockerfile Cmd Entrypoint Contract, name the daemon authority, immutable identity, process, storage, network, secret, and cleanup boundary, Dockerfile Cmd Entrypoint Contract precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Defend the transferred model · Workshop Dockerfile Cmd Entrypoint Contract, trace this concrete input and predict build, image, container, mount, network, Compose, policy, and teardown evidence before reviewing it: A workshop build sends a 780 MiB context containing .git and local.env, uses a floating base, copies the repository, runs as root, and wraps the process in /bin/sh. Redesign context and runtime contracts. Competency probe: Compose ENTRYPOINT and CMD into an override-friendly PID 1 contract and test arguments, signals, and exit status.

For Defend the transferred model · Workshop Dockerfile Cmd Entrypoint Contract, challenge the documented misconception with manifest, inspect, lifecycle, failure, repair, and transfer evidence: Multiple CMD instructions combine into a sequence of default commands.
